methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Type One Energy is a Series B-stage nuclear energy company founded by John Canik, currently valued at $200M+ on $100M+ of total raised capital. The broader nuclear sector peer universe comprises 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $380M implied valuation, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-later-stage transitions to the $200M last post-money valuation. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy subsector; while 7 same-sector peers exist broadly, the narrow filters required to ensure comparability (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) return fewer than 3 datapoints, making sector-wide median unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $323M to $437M, with a median point estimate of $380M. This range is derived entirely from the stage-progression method given peer-set constraints. Key uncertainties include the stability and applicability of the 1.9x multiplier in a nascent, capital-intensive sector where funding dynamics and technical risk profiles may diverge from broader venture patterns. Deeper granularity on comparable company funding rounds and stage definitions would strengthen confidence in this estimate.
